articles.title: Artemis II Splashes Down
articles.description: This image from April 10, 2026, captures NASA’s Orion spacecraft, with its parachutes deployed, seconds before splashdown in the Pacific Ocean. articles.title: Artemis 3 and beyond: What's next for NASA after Artemis 2 moon success
articles.description: NASA doesn't plan to rest on its laurels after the historic success of its Artemis 2 moon mission. Here are the agency's ambitious plans for Earth's nearest neighbor.

articles.title: NASA Welcomes Record-Setting Artemis II Moonfarers Back to Earth
articles.description: The first astronauts to travel to the Moon in more than half a century are back on Earth after a record-setting mission aboard NASA’s Artemis II test flight. articles.title: Splashdown! Artemis 2 astronauts return to Earth after historic NASA mission to the moon
articles.description: NASA's Artemis 2 astronauts came home today (April 10), wrapping up an epic mission that broke spaceflight records and set the stage for even more ambitious moonshots to come.

articles.title: Orion splashes down to successfully end Artemis 2 mission
articles.description: The first human mission beyond Earth orbit in more than 50 years successfully concluded with a splashdown in the Pacific Ocean April 10. The post Orion splashes down to successfully end Artemis 2 mission appeared first on SpaceNews.

articles.description: At 2:53 p.m. EDT, the Orion spacecraft ignited its thrusters for 8 seconds, producing a change in velocity of 4.2 feet-per-second and pushing Artemis II toward Earth. articles.title: The Artemis 2 astronauts saw a rare solar eclipse from beyond the moon. Here's what it looked like
articles.description: A newly released NASA timelapse reveals the moon crossing the sun as seen from deep space, giving the Artemis 2 crew a rare extended view of totality and the solar corona.

articles.title: New Perspective of Home
articles.description: Seen during Artemis II’s lunar flyby on April 6, 2026, the Moon and Earth align in the same frame, each partially illuminated by the Sun. articles.title: NASA prepares for Artemis 2 return

articles.title: Artemis II Flight Day 9: Second Return Correction Burn Complete
articles.description: At 10:53 p.m. EDT, the Orion spacecraft ignited its thrusters for 9 seconds, producing an acceleration in velocity of 5.3 feet-per-second and pushing the Artemis II crew toward Earth. The crew is now more than halfway home.

articles.title: Earthset From the Lunar Far Side
articles.description: The crew of NASA’s Artemis II mission captured extraordinary images of our home planet during their journey around the far side of the Moon.

articles.title: Starstruck
articles.description: The Artemis II crew captured this photo of our galaxy, the Milky Way, on April 7, 2026. The Milky Way’s elegant spiral structure is dominated by just two arms wrapping off the ends of a central bar of stars. articles.description: Description Staff at NASA’s Jet Propulsion Laboratory in Southern California watch the agency’s Artemis II mission unfold soon after launch on April 1, 2026, at the Space Flight Operations Facility, which operates the Deep Space Network (DSN). articles.title: Watch Artemis 2 race back to Earth in this telescope livestream tonight
articles.description: A Virtual Telescope Project livestream will track NASA's Artemis 2 Orion spacecraft as it races back toward Earth. Here's how to watch it live.

articles.title: Artemis II Flight Day 9: Crew Prepares to Come Home
articles.description: On their last full day in space, the Artemis II crew began the morning with “Lonesome Drifter” by Charley Crockett as they approached Earth at 147,337 miles.

articles.title: What returning to lunar space means for human health
articles.description: Preparing to send astronauts back to the moon is no small feat. Spaceflight exposes the body to both known and unknown risks, some of which are subtle, cumulative and difficult to predict. articles.description: Just after they broke the human spaceflight distance record, the Artemis 2 astronauts shared a powerful moment that deepened their already profound bond.

articles.title: All eyes on Orion’s heat shield: Artemis 2 astronauts will hit Earth's atmosphere at nearly 24,000 mph on April 10
articles.description: Artemis 2's Orion capsule will hit Earth's atmosphere at nearly 24,000 mph on April 10. A heat shield and 11 parachutes will help it survive the fiery trip and splash down safely.

articles.description: NASA scientists are thrilled with the Artemis 2 astronauts' moon flyby observations —especially the micrometeor impact flashes they saw.

articles.description: The Artemis 2 astronauts are capturing spectacular photos of the moon and Earth on their iPhones in space.

articles.description: Seeing Earth from space changes your perspective. And this "overview effect" is magnified when you're looking back from beyond the moon, according to Artemis 2's Christina Koch.

articles.title: Artemis II Flight Day 7: Crew Makes Long‑Distance Call, Begins Return
articles.description: After their historic lunar flyby on April 6, the Artemis II crew is awake and preparing for the journey back to Earth. articles.title: 'Unreal' solar eclipse: Artemis 2 crew just saw one of the rarest sights in spaceflight history
articles.description: Artemis 2 astronauts witness a rare solar eclipse from beyond the moon during their historic lunar flyby.

articles.title: The Artemis 2 astronauts just flew by the moon. Lunar scientists can't wait for what's next
articles.description: "Apollo happened before I was born, and the idea that we will see humans on the moon within our lifetimes, with decades of advances in science and technology behind them, is remarkable."

articles.title: Moon milestones: A rundown of Artemis 2's many spaceflight firsts
articles.description: You've probably heard it by now: NASA’s Artemis 2 mission was the first to launch astronauts to fly by the moon in more than 50 years. But that was just the first of the firsts for this mission.

articles.title: 'This is really big stuff.' Trump hails NASA Artemis 2 astronauts after historic moon flyby feat
articles.description: President Donald Trump on Monday night (April 6) hailed the courage and grit of NASA's Artemis 2 astronauts after their historic flyby around the moon.

articles.title: Artemis II Flight Day 6: Crew Wraps Historic Lunar Flyby
articles.description: The Artemis II mission wrapped up a historic seven-hour lunar flyby, marking humanity’s first return to the Moon since Apollo 17 in 1972 and capturing images of the lunar far side.
